info.title: Swings API
info.description: Swings public + admin HTTP API. Mutating endpoints are annotated; GETs are still fully callable but not listed here (see FDN-02 scope).

409.description: Pending erasure already exists, or user already erased

get.summary: Stream the artefact behind a `local`-mode async export.
get.description:
Async DSAR exports stored on R2 expose a presigned URL the operator
hits directly; for local-storage deployments (dev, single-node, or
air-gapped) we serve the JSON via this RBAC-gated route instead so
no anonymous filesystem access is required. Returns `404` for
inline jobs (the artefact is already in `artifact_url`) and for
jobs that have not yet been composed.

409.description: Job is not in a cancellable state

200.description: Subscription scheduled to cancel at period end

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Base template id (read for key/channel/locale lookup)

400.description: Order is in a terminal state

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by lifecycle state. Omit to return every row.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
Cursor — `issued_at` of the last row from the previous page.
Returns the newest page when omitted.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size, clamped to 1..=100. Defaults to 25 when omitted.

404.description: Session not found or already revoked

200.description: Policy cache reloaded; returns pair count

409.description: Refusing to drop a self-lock guard
Refusing to revoke an admin self-lock guard

parameters.name: reveal
parameters.description:
When `true` and the caller carries `admin.settings.read_secret`,
the response includes the decrypted plaintext under
`revealed_value`. Otherwise the value is redacted to `"***"`.

400.description: Validation failed (anchor must be in the future)

post.summary: Public ingest for SPA analytics (optional Bearer links session to logged-in user).

200.description: Reset email dispatched if account exists
Logged out; refresh tokens revoked. When called under an impersonation token, ends the impersonation session instead and returns `{ "message": "Impersonation ended" }`.
Verification email queued if account is pending verification

get.summary: Resolve the active banner config + category list + current policy version.
get.description:
Always returns 200 with the `default`/`en` seed if no better match exists.
The only error shape is a `503` if the tables are empty (which can only
happen if migration `024_consent.sql` has not run).

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Regulatory region override (EU, UK, US-CA, …). Usually omitted — server resolves from request headers.

400.description: Invalid action or missing subject identifier

post.summary:
POST /api/csp-report — accept a browser violation report, log it, and
return 204 No Content. Never exposes internal state or reflects the body.

get.summary: FORM-10: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country list for the chained dropdown.

FORM-10: ISO 3166-2 state / province list for the supplied alpha-2
country. Returns an empty array for uncovered countries — the
renderer falls back to a free-text input in that case.

404.description: Form not found or has no published version

post.summary: FORM-08: mint a Stripe PaymentIntent for a payment / donation field.
post.description:
Required header: `Idempotency-Key` (UUID-ish opaque token). Replays
short-circuit at the DB lookup before we ever round-trip Stripe.

422.description: Validation failed; response body carries per-field errors

200.description: Subscription scheduled to cancel at period end

requestBody.description: Resend webhook JSON payload (email.sent, email.delivered, email.bounced, email.complained, email.opened, email.clicked, email.delivery_delayed)

range.description: Echo of the resolved range so the client can rehydrate the picker.

from.description: Resolved start of the selected window (UTC, inclusive).

to.description: Resolved end of the selected window (UTC, exclusive).

previous_period.description: Counts inside the immediately-preceding window of equal length, for delta math.

AdminUpdatePricingPlanResponse.description: Response for admin plan update — catalog row plus optional Stripe rollout stats.

AllowlistEntry.description: Materialised allowlist entry as returned by the admin CRUD endpoints.

cidr.description:
CIDR range in canonical text form (e.g. `203.0.113.0/24`,
`2001:db8::/32`). Always returned via `cidr::text` cast on read.

label.description:
Human label so operators can recall what each entry covers
(e.g. "office VPN", "Cloudflare egress").

version.description:
Banner config row version. Bumped when copy/layout changes; used by
CONSENT-03 to re-prompt subjects whose recorded consent predates this.

theme.description: Opaque theme overrides; the frontend maps known keys to PE7 CSS vars.

BogoConfig.description:
Configuration for a "buy N, get M free" promotion.

The engine groups cart lines matching `applies_to_product_ids` (empty = any
product), totals their quantity, and emits `floor(total / (buy + get)) * get`
free units, evaluated at the cheapest matching unit price so the customer's
"free" units come off the least-expensive line first. That rule mirrors
WooCommerce's default BOGO behaviour and avoids the abuse of stacking a
high-priced free unit onto a low-priced purchase.

BundleItem.description:
`bundle_items` row. `quantity` is always positive (CHECK-enforced).
`child_variant_id` is optional; when set, the bundle includes that specific
variant, otherwise it includes the default/any variant of the child product.

BundleItemInput.description: Admin-only payload for a single bundle component.

duration_days.description:
Length of the comp grant in days; `None` ⇒ open-ended (the
access engine treats `ends_at IS NULL` as "until cancelled").

notes.description: Free-text reason captured on the audit row.

ConsentCategoryDef.description:
Single-category entry in the banner response.

Wire fields are camelCased to match the frontend stub at
`src/lib/api/consent.ts`; when schema codegen replaces the stub the
Svelte components consume this shape unchanged.

key.description:
Stable key — MUST NOT be renamed after a row has been written to the
consent log (CONSENT-03). Migration-level change only.

required.description: When true, toggle is disabled in the preferences modal.

defaultEnabled.description:
Whether the category is pre-checked before the user interacts.
GDPR Art. 4(11) + EDPB 05/2020 §86: non-required categories MUST default
to `false`. Derived here rather than stored so a data fix is cheap.

action.description:
One of `granted` / `denied` / `updated` / `revoked` / `expired` /
`prefill`. Enforced at the DB CHECK level; validated here for a
friendlier 400.

categories.description:
Map of category key → granted bool. Must include every category the
current banner version exposes.

services.description:
Optional per-service overrides (when the subject used the advanced
picker). Empty object is fine.

anonymousId.description:
Browser-generated anonymous id (UUID cookie). Used when the subject
is not signed in so the audit log can still be linked across sessions.

bannerVersion.description:
Optional banner / policy version overrides — defaults come from the
authoritative banner config when omitted. Kept as options so the
client can send them when it already has a cached banner copy.

ConsentRecordRow.description:
Serialized row shape — shared across `POST /record` response and
`GET /me` listings.

CouponEngineView.description:
Row shape covering the EC-11 fields plus the legacy `is_active` flag so
the response mirrors the admin's view after mutation.

CouponScope.description: Discount scope — where on the cart the discount applies.

cidr.description:
CIDR range. Anything Postgres' `cidr` type accepts is valid here
(`192.0.2.42`, `198.51.100.0/24`, `::1/128`, …).

is_active.description: Optional initial active flag — defaults to `true`.

CreateAssetRequest.description: Admin-only payload for adding a downloadable asset.

target_user_id.description:
User to impersonate. Must exist; must not be the caller; must
not itself be an admin (defence-in-depth — `impersonator` audit
trail across admins would be a privilege-escalation footgun).

reason.description:
Required free-text justification (1..=500 chars). Surfaced in
the audit log so post-incident review can answer "why".

ttl_minutes.description:
Requested TTL in minutes. Capped at [`MAX_TTL_MINUTES`].
Defaults to [`DEFAULT_TTL_MINUTES`] when omitted.

role.description:
Role to seed the account with. Admin is rejected — the
operator must escalate via the dedicated role-update endpoint
so the audit trail flags the event explicitly.

temp_password.description:
Optional one-shot temporary password. When `None` the account
is created in a disabled state (no login until the user
completes the password-reset / invite flow).

email_verified.description:
Mark the email as already verified (operator vouches that
they typed the address themselves, e.g. seeding a colleague).

requires_password_setup.description:
`true` when no temp_password was supplied and the operator
must follow up with a password-reset / invite link out of band.

CreateProductRequest.description:
Admin-only payload for `POST /api/admin/products`. `product_type` + `slug`
are mandatory; everything else is optional on create and can be updated
later.

CreateVariantRequest.description: Admin-only payload for creating a variant.

anchor.description:
New `billing_cycle_anchor`. Must be in the future (you cannot
retroactively shift a billing cycle in this admin path; if you
need to backdate, do it via Stripe and let the webhook
reconcile).

DashboardRange.description:
Selectable reporting window for `GET /api/admin/stats`.

All variants except `Custom` resolve to a window ending at `now` and
extending back the indicated duration (or to the start of the current UTC
year for `YearToDate`). `Custom` requires `from` + `to` `YYYY-MM-DD` query
params (inclusive `to`).

oneOf.description: Reporting window. Defaults to `last_30_days` when omitted.

from.description: Inclusive start `YYYY-MM-DD`. Required when `range=custom`, ignored otherwise.

to.description: Inclusive end `YYYY-MM-DD`. Required when `range=custom`, ignored otherwise.

DownloadableAsset.description:
`downloadable_assets` row. `storage_key` is the R2 object key; EC-07 will
wire signed-URL issuance against it. `access_policy` gates who can request
a fresh signed URL.

DsarFulfillResponse.description:
Admin fulfill response. When the DSAR is `access` / `portability` and the
admin did not provide a URL, the exported JSON is inlined in `export` so
the caller can pipe it to a download without a second round-trip.

artifact_kind.description:
ADM-17: storage transport for the artefact. `inline`
(legacy synchronous path → `data:` URI on `artifact_url`),
`r2` (presigned URL on `artifact_url`), or `local`
(download via `/jobs/{id}/artifact` streamer with bearer
auth).

artifact_storage_key.description:
ADM-17: opaque storage key (R2 object key, or `dsar/{id}.json`
for local mode). Operator surface only — downloads use
`artifact_url` or the streamer.

artifact_expires_at.description:
ADM-17 + ADM-19: TTL after which the TTL sweep deletes the
underlying object and NULLs all three artefact columns.

DsarRow.description: Row shape returned from the admin list + the subject submission response.

async.description:
ADM-17: opt into the async pipeline. When `true` the handler
queues a `pending` job for the background worker and returns
`202 Accepted` with the row but no inline export. When `false`
(default) the legacy synchronous compose runs inline and the
response includes the full document — preserved for parity with
pre-ADM-17 callers and for ergonomic small-export UX.

export.description:
Inline JSON snapshot of the export. Operators usually consume
`job.artifact_url` (a `data:` URI for inline mode, presigned R2
URL or `/artifact` streamer route for async mode) for the
round-trippable document; `export` is the same payload
deserialised so admin UIs can render it without a second hop.
`null` when the request opted into the async pipeline because
the worker has not composed the artefact yet.

days.description: Number of days to add to `current_period_end`. Must be 1..=366.

FormDefinition.description: Payload of `GET /api/forms/{slug}` — the shape FORM-10's renderer hydrates.

schema.description: Active version's `schema_json` — a JSON array of `FieldSchema` variants.

logic.description: Active version's `logic_json` — a JSON array of `LogicRule`.

ImpersonationSession.description:
Materialised impersonation-session row as returned by the admin CRUD
endpoints and consumed by [`crate::extractors::AuthUser`].

ip_address.description:
Mint-time IP in canonical text form. Stored as Postgres `inet`,
projected via `host(...)` so callers don't need the `ipnetwork`
sqlx feature.

reason.description:
Optional free-text reason captured in the audit log + the `users`
row (truncated to 512 chars by the DB CHECK constraint).

next_cursor.description:
Cursor (`issued_at` of the last row) to pass back via `?after=`
to fetch the next page; `None` when the page is not full.

unit_price_cents.description:
Cents per unit. Operator-supplied so wholesale / comp pricing
can deviate from the public catalogue.

mark_completed.description:
`true` ⇒ skip the pending state and mark the order completed
immediately (the operator already collected payment off-band).
`false` (default) leaves the order in `pending` so the standard
reconciliation pipeline can advance it.

matrix.description:
Every active (role, permission) row, alphabetised by (role,
permission). Stable order — the SPA renders directly.

roles.description:
Every role label the backend recognises. Driven by the Rust
enum so the SPA does not need to mirror it.

permissions.description: Every permission key in the catalogue.

MintResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /` (mint). Contains the freshly-issued
impersonation JWT plus the row metadata so the SPA does not need a
follow-up GET to render the banner.

expires_at.description: Echoed `expires_at` — same value carried in the JWT's `exp`.

categories.description: Flattened category → granted map, derived from the most recent record.

decidedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC. `None` if the subject has no recorded decisions yet.

NotificationPreference.description:
A (user, category, channel) preference row as stored in
`notification_preferences`.

OutboxRowDto.description:
Paginated outbox row. Kept in sync with [`OutboxRecord`] but serialises the
status as a lowercase string for frontend friendliness.

OutboxStatus.description:
Lifecycle states for an outbox row. Mirrors the `CHECK` constraint in
`019_outbox.sql`.

PaginatedOutboxResponse.description:
OpenAPI wrapper so the snapshot carries a concrete `PaginatedResponse<OutboxRowDto>`
schema without bleeding the generic into `ApiDoc`.

suspended_at.description:
ADM-02: lifecycle state surfaced to the admin members UI. `None`
for each timestamp means the account is not in that state.

resume_token.description:
Optional: extend an existing resume token in place. When set + the
token is valid, the existing row is updated and the same token is
returned; otherwise a fresh token is minted.

current_step.description:
Zero-based page-break index the draft was on when saved. Lets the
renderer jump straight back to the right step on resume.

field_key.description: FieldSchema `key` of the payment field on the form.

amount_cents.description:
Donor-supplied amount for `payment_kind = donation`. Ignored for
fixed-amount one-time payments — the schema's `amount_cents` wins.

email.description: Donor email — receipts are sent to this address.

resume_token.description:
Optional resume token if the field is on a draft (so we can
later cross-link `form_payment_intents.partial_id`).

PeriodWindow.description:
Per-window counts. The dashboard sends two of these — the selected window
and the immediately-preceding window of identical length — so the UI can
compute deltas without a second round-trip.

PreferenceUpdate.description: Input shape for the bulk set endpoint. Missing timezone defaults to UTC.

push_to_stripe_subscriptions.description:
When `true`, after the catalog row is persisted the API updates each
targeted Stripe subscription’s primary line item to the new amount or
`stripe_price_id`. Requires an `Idempotency-Key` request header.

PricingStripeRolloutAudience.description: Controls whether saving a catalog plan also mutates existing Stripe subscriptions.

Product.description:
`products` row. Money fields are `BIGINT` cents; translate via
[`crate::common::money::Money::cents`] when arithmetic is needed.

ProductDetail.description:
Bundle of a product + its first-class children. Returned by `GET
/api/admin/products/{id}` and `GET /api/products/{slug}` so the admin UI
and the public PDP both receive a single hydrated document.

ProductStatus.description:
Publication lifecycle. `draft` is the default on insert; only `published`
rows surface through the public router. `archived` is terminal and hides
the product from every lookup except admin list views.

ProductType.description:
Top-level product archetype. Matches the CHECK constraint in
`migrations/040_products.sql`.

ProductVariant.description:
`product_variants` row. A variant overrides product pricing when
`price_cents` is set; otherwise it inherits.

RecurringMode.description: Subscription billing applicability for a coupon.

refresh_token.description:
Optional during BFF rollout (Phase 1.3): when the SPA carries the
refresh token via the `swings_refresh` httpOnly cookie, the JSON body
is empty (`{}`) and this field is `None`. The handler reads from the
cookie jar in that case. Legacy clients can still send the value here.

stripe_refund_id.description:
Operator-supplied Stripe refund id when the refund was issued
out-of-band via the dashboard. Persisted on `order_refunds`
so reconciliation can match.

order_marked_refunded.description:
`true` when the cumulative refund total reached the order
total and the engine flipped the order to `refunded`.

RenderedTemplate.description:
Output of [`Template::render`]. Kept narrow so channels do not have to
re-parse Tera output.

permissions.description:
New permission set for the role. Unknown keys (not in the
permissions catalogue) are rejected with `400`.

reason.description:
Optional free-text justification for the revocation. Stored on
the row and surfaced in the audit-log metadata.

oneOf.description:
Set only when `?reveal=true` succeeded. JSON value (string for
`secret`, native shape otherwise).

SettingRecord.description:
Materialised in-memory representation of an `app_settings` row.
`value` always carries the canonical Postgres JSONB; for `Secret`
rows the JSON is the encrypted envelope (see `crypto`).

SettingType.description:
Storage discriminator. Mirrors the `value_type` CHECK constraint in
migration 062. Kept as a strict enum so the admin handler cannot
silently coerce between types.

oneOf.description:
Required when creating a new key. Ignored on update of an
existing key (the stored type is canonical).

is_secret.description: Defaults to `false` on create. Ignored on update.

description.description: Optional admin-facing description; only persisted on create.

category.description: Defaults to `"general"` on create. Ignored on update.

value.description:
The raw value. For `secret` types, pass the cleartext as a JSON
string — the server encrypts before persisting.

SettingView.description:
Public-API view: redacts secret values and surfaces the canonical
envelope structure. Use [`reveal_secret`] when the caller carries
`admin.settings.read_secret`.

value.description:
Either the actual value (non-secret) or the JSON literal
`"***"` (secret + caller lacks reveal permission).

utm.description: Optional UTM block captured from the query-string at render time.

files.description:
Optional file descriptors from FORM-05. Shape `[{ field_key, file_id,
filename, size, sha256, mime_type }]`.

anonymous_id.description:
Optional anonymous id cookie — the frontend generates one for
unauthenticated sessions so repeat submissions tie back together.

pricing_plan_id.description:
When set, this subscription was purchased from a specific `pricing_plans`
catalog row (via Checkout metadata → Stripe subscription metadata → webhooks).

Template.description:
A concrete template row loaded from `notification_templates`.

`body_compiled` is maintained by the admin API at save time (currently a
straight copy of `body_source` until MJML compilation lands in FDN-09);
rendering reads `body_compiled` so the hot path never re-parses MJML.

TombstoneSummary.description:
Summary of a tombstone operation.

Persisted on `dsar_jobs.erasure_summary` so audits can verify the
service did exactly what it claimed.

refresh_tokens_deleted.description: Row counts of auxiliary tables we cleared.

UpdateCouponEngineRequest.description:
Admin-only payload for `PUT /api/admin/coupons/{id}/engine`. Every field
is optional; `None` means "leave the existing value alone". Pass an empty
array to clear an includes/excludes list.

discount_value_cents.description:
Flat discount value in minor units (cents). Setting both this and
`discount_percent_bps` is accepted but only one takes effect — the
engine prefers the cents field.

discount_percent_bps.description: Percentage discount in basis points (`10_000 bps = 100%`).

UpdateProductRequest.description:
Admin-only payload for `PUT /api/admin/products/{id}`. Every field is
optional — `None` means "leave the existing value alone" (COALESCE in the
repo layer). `product_type` is deliberately not update-able because
changing it post-creation would invalidate variant + asset + bundle rows.

UpdateVariantRequest.description: Admin-only payload for updating a variant.

suspended_at.description:
ADM-02: lifecycle state surfaced to the admin members UI. `None`
for each timestamp means the account is not in that state.

memberships.description: All non-expired memberships attached to the user, newest first.

ValidationError.description:
Structured validation failure. Stable `code` strings are the shared contract
with `src/lib/forms/validate.ts`.

tags.name: consent
tags.description: Cookie / tracking consent banner + category lookup

tags.name: admin-security
tags.description: Admin-only privileged member-lifecycle and security console (suspend, ban, sessions, audit log)

tags.name: admin-impersonation
tags.description: Admin-only impersonation token mint / list / revoke

tags.name: admin-settings
tags.description: Admin-only typed settings catalogue (incl. maintenance-mode kill-switch)

tags.name: admin-roles
tags.description: Admin-only role / permission matrix CRUD with hot policy reload

tags.name: admin-subscriptions
tags.description: Admin-only manual subscription operations (comp, extend, billing-cycle override)

tags.name: admin-orders
tags.description: Admin-only orders surface (list, manual create, void, partial refund, CSV export)

tags.name: admin-dsar
tags.description: Admin-initiated DSAR jobs (export + dual-control right-to-erasure tombstone)

tags.name: admin-audit
tags.description: Audit log viewer with full-text + JSON-path search over admin_actions
